questions.question: setState() can be called from any method in a StatefulWidget.
questions.explanation: Yes, but it should only be called when the widget is currently mounted.

questions.question: Navigator.push() adds a new route to the stack.
questions.explanation: Correct, it pushes a new screen onto the navigation stack.

questions.question: Form widgets automatically validate on save.
questions.explanation: Validation is triggered manually via FormState.validate().

questions.question: Provider is a state management solution that uses InheritedWidget.
questions.explanation: Yes, Provider is built on top of InheritedWidget.

questions.question: Hot reload works when you change native iOS code.
questions.explanation: Hot reload only applies to Dart code. Native changes require a full rebuild.

questions.question: The 'async' keyword makes a function return a Future.
questions.explanation: Yes, an async function automatically returns a Future.

questions.question: All widgets in Flutter are immutable.
questions.explanation: StatefulWidgets have mutable state, but the widget itself is still immutable.

questions.question: You can use a Theme to apply consistent styling across your app.
questions.explanation: ThemeData defines colors, fonts, and shapes for the entire app.
